name: Compose Dependency Ordering
description: Configure service startup order in Docker Compose using depends_on with health check conditions to prevent race conditions.

steps.title: Define health checks for dependency services
steps.description: Ensure database and cache services have health checks so dependent services know when they are ready.
steps.command:
cat <<'EOF'
services:
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
EOF

steps.title: Configure depends_on with conditions
steps.description: Make the API service wait until the database is healthy before starting.
steps.command:
cat <<'EOF'
services:
  api:
    image: myapp:latest
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
EOF
steps.tip: Without the condition, depends_on only waits for the container to start, not for the service inside to be ready.

steps.title: Verify startup order
steps.description: Start the stack and verify services come up in the correct order.
steps.command: docker compose up -d && docker compose ps

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Compose Dependency Ordering Check
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Service Status and Dependencies ==="
docker compose ps --format 'table {{.Name}}\t{{.Status}}'

echo ""
echo "=== Compose Config Validation ==="
docker compose config --quiet && echo "Config is valid" || echo "Config has errors"

echo ""
echo "=== Startup Order Tips ==="
echo "1. Add healthcheck to every database/cache service"
echo "2. Use depends_on with condition: service_healthy"
echo "3. Set appropriate start_period for slow-starting services"
echo "4. Consider retry logic in application code as a fallback"

faq.question: Should I rely only on depends_on for ordering?
faq.answer: No. Always implement connection retry logic in your application as well. depends_on helps with initial startup, but services can become temporarily unavailable at any time.

faq.question: What is the difference between service_started and service_healthy?
faq.answer: service_started only waits for the container to be running. service_healthy waits until the health check passes, meaning the service inside is actually ready.
